title: dogcheck
desc: A web recreation of the DOGCHECK screen from Toby Fox's games — URL parameters let you override the message, or show an HTTP/Chrome error code with its official name. 5% chance of an easter egg (DELTARUNE Chapter 4 spoilers).

title: robo-queue
desc: A small Python program built to help with robotics competition prep. Uses text-to-speech to announce the names.

title: SCP_Data
desc: A terminal UI that displays articles straight from the official SCP Wiki. You have to add SCP entries by yourself.

title: ttsScript
desc: A friendly text-to-speech script built on pyttsx3. Run this on a Windows machine for maximum compatibility.

title: animeClassPython
desc: Ran out of project ideas mid-way through this one. Probably easier to just go read the code. It's still somehow actively editied

title: EulerProjectAttempts
desc: Ongoing attempts at the Project Euler archive of problems — open-ended by nature, so this one never really finishes. (I'm aiming to finish 100 questions)

title: pythonTest
desc: A script where you can run all of your Python endavours. Experiment with your code without the normal 'Traceback (most recent call last)'.

title: portfolio
desc: A Portfolio website in the design of a operating system with a POST screen, a BIOS and Asset loading resembling to Arch Linux. It's a huge project and will likely slow me down on other ones. Currently in version 1 and you are currently viewing it.

title: musicTrasition
desc: A calm to combat music transition inspired by ULTRAKILL. Using PyGame and Tkinker.

title: ultrakillStyle
desc: A recreation of the ULTRAKILL style meter in the terminal that reacts to what you do on your device.
